We prompt the model with a 32×32323232\times 32 grid on the first frame, and additionally we use a 16×16161616\times 16 grid on 4 zoomed image crops of the first frame (derived from a 2×2222\times 2 overlapped window) and a 4×4444\times 4 grid on 16 zoomed image crops of the first frame (derived from a 4×4444\times 4 overlapped window). We apply two post-processing steps across all frames. First, we remove tiny disconnected components with areas smaller than 200 pixels. Second, we fill in holes in segmentation masks if the area of the hole is less than 200 pixels. By combining these automatically generated masklets with manually created ones, we enhance the coverage of annotations in the SA-V dataset, as illustrated in Fig. 10.
items.zh: 自动掩码。类似于 Kirillov et al. (2023) 描述的方法，自动掩码通过使用规则网格提示模型生成。我们在第一帧上使用 32×32 网格提示模型，此外在第一帧的 4 个缩放图像裁剪（来自 2×2 重叠窗口）上使用 16×16 网格，并在第一帧的 16 个缩放图像裁剪（来自 4×4 重叠窗口）上使用 4×4 网格。我们对所有帧应用两个后处理步骤。首先，移除面积小于 200 像素的微小断开组件。其次，如果空洞面积小于 200 像素，则填充分割掩码中的空洞。通过将这些自动生成的掩码与手动创建的掩码相结合，我们增强了 SA-V 数据集中标注的覆盖范围，如图 10 所示。

items.capEn: Figure 11: Annotation guideline overview. There are 3 main annotation tasks: masklet selection, masklet tracking, and masklet verification. Each task has a different set of annotators working on it.
items.capZh: 图 11：标注指南概述。有 3 个主要标注任务：掩码选择、掩码跟踪和掩码验证。每个任务由不同的标注人员组执行。

items.en: A diagram of the annotation protocol used in our data engine is shown in Fig. 11. The annotation task was separated into steps each carried out by a different annotator: Steps 1 and 2 focus on object selection, Steps 3 and 4 on masklet tracking, and Step 5 on quality verification. SAM 2 was deployed on GPU as an API and built into the annotation tool to enable interactive use.
items.zh: 数据引擎中使用的标注协议图如图 11 所示。标注任务被分为多个步骤，每个步骤由不同的标注人员执行：步骤 1 和 2 侧重于对象选择，步骤 3 和 4 侧重于掩码跟踪，步骤 5 侧重于质量验证。SAM 2 作为 API 部署在 GPU 上，并集成到标注工具中以实现交互式使用。

items.en: Compared to image segmentation annotation, large-scale video segmentation annotation presents unique challenges which require innovations in the annotation task design and protocol. To improve our model’s ability to “segment anything”, it was important to focus annotation on challenging objects where SAM 2 struggled. We leveraged our online model in the loop setup to enable this, requesting annotators to use SAM 2 interactively to identify failure modes and then correct them.
items.zh: 与图像分割标注相比，大规模视频分割标注提出了独特的挑战，需要在标注任务设计和协议上进行创新。为了提高模型“分割任何物体”的能力，将标注重点放在 SAM 2 难以处理的挑战性对象上非常重要。我们利用在线模型在环设置来实现这一点，要求标注人员交互式地使用 SAM 2 来识别失败模式，然后进行修正。

items.en: We found the number of edited frames to be a proxy to the “challengingness” of an object as shown in Table 9. Therefore, we asked annotators to annotate objects that required at least 2 edited frames with SAM 2 in the loop. To focus annotation on less prominent and more challenging cases, annotators were presented with videos pre-filled with verified satisfactory automatic masklets and asked to find un-annotated challenging objects. We further decouple the object selection task from the annotation task: in the selection task annotators focus on choosing the challenging objects in one frame, while in the annotation task annotators are presented with a challenging target object and requested to annotate the masklet consistently throughout the video.
items.zh: 我们发现编辑帧的数量可以作为对象“挑战性”的代理，如表 9 所示。因此，我们要求标注人员标注那些在 SAM 2 在环下需要至少 2 个编辑帧的对象。为了将标注重点放在不太突出和更具挑战性的案例上，标注人员会看到预先填充了经过验证的满意自动掩码的视频，并被要求找到未标注的挑战性对象。我们进一步将对象选择任务与标注任务解耦：在选择任务中，标注人员专注于在一帧中选择挑战性对象，而在标注任务中，标注人员会看到一个挑战性目标对象，并被要求在整个视频中一致地标注掩码。

items.en: The comparison of data engine phases shown in Table 1 was conducted as a controlled experiment using 169 videos and 452 masklets. We ask three subsets of annotators to annotate the same set of objects with the annotation protocol from each phase. We categorize masklets into three buckets based on the mask area in the first frame (small: 1 to 322superscript32232^{2}, medium: 322superscript32232^{2} to 962superscript96296^{2}, and large: equal or greater than 962superscript96296^{2}). Phase 1 data is used as the quality reference, due to the high quality masks from frame-by-frame manual annotation with SAM.
items.zh: 表 1 中显示的数据引擎阶段比较是作为对照实验进行的，使用了 169 个视频和 452 个掩码。我们要求三组标注人员使用每个阶段的标注协议标注同一组对象。我们根据第一帧中的掩码面积将掩码分为三类（小：1 到 32²，中：32² 到 96²，大：大于等于 96²）。阶段 1 的数据用作质量参考，因为使用 SAM 进行逐帧手动标注产生了高质量掩码。

full.sec: Appendix E Details on zero-shot transfer experiments
full.secZh: 附录E 零样本迁移实验细节

items.en: In this section, we describe further details of our zero-shot experiments (§6). Unless otherwise noted, the results reported in this section follow our default setup using Hiera-B+ image encoder with a resolution of 1024 and trained on the full combination of datasets, i.e., SAM 2 (Hiera-B+) in Table 7.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们描述了零样本实验（§6）的更多细节。除非另有说明，本节报告的结果遵循我们的默认设置，使用 Hiera-B+ 图像编码器，分辨率为 1024，并在完整数据集组合上训练，即表 7 中的 SAM 2 (Hiera-B+)。

items.en: We evaluate SAM 2 on a diverse benchmark of 17 zero-shot datasets: EndoVis 2018 (Allan et al., 2020) contains medical surgery videos with robotic instruments. ESD (Huang et al., 2023) contains videos from a robot manipulator camera often with motion blur. LVOSv2 (Hong et al., 2024) is a benchmark for long-term video object segmentation. LV-VIS (Wang et al., 2023) contains videos from a diverse set of open-vocabulary object categories. UVO (Wang et al., 2021b) contains videos for open-world object segmentation, and VOST (Tokmakov et al., 2022) contains videos with objects undergoing large transformations such as egg broken or paper torn. PUMaVOS (Bekuzarov et al., 2023) contains videos with segments around object parts such as a person’s cheek. Virtual KITTI 2 (Cabon et al., 2020) is a synthetic video dataset with driving scenes. VIPSeg (Miao et al., 2022) provides object segmentation in panoptic videos. Wildfires (Toulouse et al., 2017) contains wildfire videos under different conditions from the Corsican Fire Database. VISOR (Darkhalil et al., 2022) contains egocentric videos in kitchen scenes with segments around hands and active objects. FBMS (Brox et al., 2010) provides motion segmentation over moving objects in videos. Ego-Exo4D (Grauman et al., 2023) is a large dataset with egocentric videos around various human activities. Cityscapes (Cordts et al., 2016) contains videos for urban driving scenes. Lindenthal Camera (Haucke & Steinhage, 2021) contains videos in a wildlife park with segments around observed animals such as birds and mammals. HT1080WT Cells (Gómez-de Mariscal et al., 2021) contains microscopy videos with cell segments. Drosophila Heart (Fishman et al., 2023) contains microscopy videos for the heart of fruit flies.
items.zh: 我们在 17 个零样本数据集的多样化基准上评估 SAM 2：EndoVis 2018（Allan et al., 2020）包含带有机器人器械的医学手术视频。ESD（Huang et al., 2023）包含来自机器人操纵器摄像头的视频，通常带有运动模糊。LVOSv2（Hong et al., 2024）是长期视频对象分割的基准。LV-VIS（Wang et al., 2023）包含来自多种开放词汇对象类别的视频。UVO（Wang et al., 2021b）包含用于开放世界对象分割的视频，VOST（Tokmakov et al., 2022）包含对象经历大变换（如鸡蛋破碎或纸张撕裂）的视频。PUMaVOS（Bekuzarov et al., 2023）包含对象部分（如人的脸颊）周围的分段视频。Virtual KITTI 2（Cabon et al., 2020）是带有驾驶场景的合成视频数据集。VIPSeg（Miao et al., 2022）在全景视频中提供对象分割。Wildfires（Toulouse et al., 2017）包含来自科西嘉火灾数据库的不同条件下的野火视频。VISOR（Darkhalil et al., 2022）包含厨房场景中的自我中心视频，分段围绕手和活动对象。FBMS（Brox et al., 2010）提供视频中运动对象的分割。Ego-Exo4D（Grauman et al., 2023）是一个大型数据集，包含围绕各种人类活动的自我中心视频。Cityscapes（Cordts et al., 2016）包含城市驾驶场景的视频。Lindenthal Camera（Haucke & Steinhage, 2021）包含野生动物公园中的视频，分段围绕观察到的动物，如鸟类和哺乳动物。HT1080WT Cells（Gómez-de Mariscal et al., 2021）包含显微镜视频，带有细胞分段。Drosophila Heart（Fishman et al., 2023）包含果蝇心脏的显微镜视频。

items.en: Among these 17 zero-shot video datasets above, 9 of them (EndoVis, ESD, LVOSv2, LV-VIS, UVO, VOST, PUMaVOS, Virtual KITTI 2, and VIPSeg) have dense object segments annotated for every video frame. In the remaining 8 datasets (Wildfires, VISOR, FBMS, Ego-Exo4D, Cityscapes, Lindenthal Camera, HT1080WT Cells, and Drosophila Heart), the object segments are sparsely annotated over only a subset of video frames, and we compute the metrics on those frames where the ground-truth segmentation masks are available. In most evaluations of the paper, we only evaluate zero-shot performance on the 9 densely annotated datasets, while in our semi-supervised VOS evaluation (§6.1.2), we evaluate on all these 17 datasets listed above.
items.zh: 在上述 17 个零样本视频数据集中，其中 9 个（EndoVis、ESD、LVOSv2、LV-VIS、UVO、VOST、PUMaVOS、Virtual KITTI 2 和 VIPSeg）为每个视频帧标注了密集的对象分段。在其余 8 个数据集（Wildfires、VISOR、FBMS、Ego-Exo4D、Cityscapes、Lindenthal Camera、HT1080WT Cells 和 Drosophila Heart）中，对象分段仅在部分视频帧上稀疏标注，我们在那些具有真实分割掩码的帧上计算指标。在论文的大多数评估中，我们仅评估 9 个密集标注数据集的零样本性能，而在半监督 VOS 评估（§6.1.2）中，我们评估上述所有 17 个数据集。

items.en: Offline evaluation involves multiple passes over the entire video. We start with click prompts on the first frame, segment the object throughout the entire video, and then in the next pass, we select the frame with the lowest segmentation IoUIoU\mathrm{IoU} w.r.t. the ground-truth as the new frame for prompting. The model then segments the object again throughout the video based on all prompts received previously, until reaching a maximum of Nframesubscript𝑁frameN_{\mathrm{frame}} passes (with one new prompted frame in each pass).
items.zh: 离线评估涉及对整个视频进行多次遍历。我们从第一帧上的点击提示开始，在整个视频中分割对象，然后在下一遍中，选择相对于真实值具有最低分割 IoU 的帧作为新的提示帧。模型然后基于之前接收到的所有提示再次分割整个视频，直到达到最大 N_frame 次遍历（每次遍历中有一个新的提示帧）。

items.en: Online evaluation involves only one pass over the entire video. We start with click prompts on the first frame and propagate the prompts across the video, pausing propagation when encountering a frame with a low-quality prediction (IoU<0.75IoU0.75\mathrm{IoU}<0.75 with ground-truth). We then add additional click prompts on the paused frame to correct the segment on this frame and resume the propagation forward until reaching another low quality frame with IoU<0.75IoU0.75\mathrm{IoU}<0.75. This is repeated while the number of prompted frames is less than the maximum Nframesubscript𝑁frameN_{\mathrm{frame}}. Unlike the previous offline evaluation, in this setting, the new prompts only affect the frames after the current paused frame but not the frames before it.
items.zh: 在线评估仅对整个视频进行一次遍历。我们从第一帧的点击提示开始，并在视频中传播提示，当遇到预测质量较低的帧（与真实标注的IoU<0.75）时暂停传播。然后，我们在暂停的帧上添加额外的点击提示以修正该帧的分割，并继续向前传播，直到再次遇到IoU<0.75的低质量帧。重复此过程，直到提示帧数小于最大帧数N_frame。与之前的离线评估不同，在此设置中，新提示仅影响当前暂停帧之后的帧，而不影响其之前的帧。

items.en: In both settings, we evaluate on 9 densely annotated datasets in §E.1.1 (EndoVis, ESD, LVOSv2, LV-VIS, UVO, VOST, PUMaVOS, Virtual KITTI 2, and VIPSeg). If a video contains multiple objects to segment in its ground-truth annotations, we perform inference on each object independently. We simulate interactive video segmentation with Nclick=3subscript𝑁click3N_{\mathrm{click}}=3 clicks per frame, assuming that the user would visually locate the object to label it (with initial clicks) or to refine the current segmentation prediction of it (with correction clicks). Specifically, when starting the first pass (where there are not any existing predictions yet), we place an initial click on the first frame at the center111The center of a mask is defined as the mask pixel that has the largest Euclidean distance to the mask boundary. of the object’s ground-truth mask and then interactively add two more clicks based on the center of the error region (between the ground-truth mask and the predicted segments on the first frame). Then in subsequent passes (where there are already predicted segments), we interactively add three clicks based on the center of the error region (between the ground-truth mask and the predicted segments on the frame being prompted).
items.zh: 在两种设置中，我们在§E.1.1中的9个密集标注数据集（EndoVis、ESD、LVOSv2、LV-VIS、UVO、VOST、PUMaVOS、Virtual KITTI 2和VIPSeg）上进行评估。如果一个视频的真实标注中包含多个要分割的对象，我们对每个对象独立进行推理。我们模拟交互式视频分割，每帧使用N_click=3次点击，假设用户会通过视觉定位对象来标注（初始点击）或修正当前的分割预测（修正点击）。具体来说，在开始第一次遍历（尚无任何预测）时，我们在第一帧的对象真实标注掩码中心放置一个初始点击，然后根据误差区域中心（第一帧上真实标注掩码与预测分割之间的区域）交互式地添加两次点击。在后续遍历（已有预测分割）中，我们根据误差区域中心（被提示帧上真实标注掩码与预测分割之间的区域）交互式地添加三次点击。

items.en: We report the average 𝒥&ℱ𝒥ℱ\mathcal{J}\&\mathcal{F} metric over Nframe=1,…,8subscript𝑁frame1…8N_{\mathrm{frame}}=1,\ldots,8 interacted frames and the 𝒥&ℱ𝒥ℱ\mathcal{J}\&\mathcal{F} metrics under different annotation time on a video based on the following assumptions:
items.zh: 我们报告在N_frame=1,…,8个交互帧上的平均𝒥&ℱ指标，以及基于以下假设在不同标注时间下的𝒥&ℱ指标：

items.en: On each frame, it takes Tloc=1subscript𝑇loc1T_{\mathrm{loc}}=1 sec for the annotator to visually locate an object in the frame, and Tclick=1.5subscript𝑇click1.5T_{\mathrm{click}}=1.5 sec to add each click, following Delatolas et al. (2024).
items.zh: 在每帧上，标注者视觉定位对象需要T_loc=1秒，每次点击需要T_click=1.5秒，遵循Delatolas等人（2024）的方法。

items.en: In offline mode, it takes Texam=30subscript𝑇exam30T_{\mathrm{exam}}=30 sec on a 300-frame video to examine the results throughout the video in each round, including finding the frame with the worst segmentation quality to add corrections (and for longer or shorter videos, this time is proportional to the video length L𝐿L, assuming the annotator could examine the results at 10 FPS).
items.zh: 在离线模式下，对于300帧的视频，每轮检查整个视频的结果需要T_exam=30秒，包括找到分割质量最差的帧以添加修正（对于更长或更短的视频，该时间与视频长度L成正比，假设标注者能以10 FPS的速度检查结果）。

items.en: In online mode, it takes Texam=30subscript𝑇exam30T_{\mathrm{exam}}=30 sec on a 300-frame video to follow the results throughout the video in total, including pausing at a frame with low quality for further corrections (and this time is proportional to the video length L𝐿L similar to the offline mode).
items.zh: 在在线模式下，对于300帧的视频，全程跟踪结果需要T_exam=30秒，包括在低质量帧处暂停以进行进一步修正（该时间与视频长度L成正比，类似于离线模式）。

items.en: The annotation time for an object is (Texam⋅(L/300)+Tloc+Tclick⋅Nclick)⋅Nframe⋅⋅subscript𝑇exam𝐿300subscript𝑇loc⋅subscript𝑇clicksubscript𝑁clicksubscript𝑁frame\left(T_{\mathrm{exam}}\cdot\left(L/300\right)+T_{\mathrm{loc}}+T_{\mathrm{click}}\cdot N_{\mathrm{click}}\right)\cdot N_{\mathrm{frame}} in offline mode and Texam⋅(L/300)+(Tloc+Tclick⋅Nclick)⋅Nframe⋅subscript𝑇exam𝐿300⋅subscript𝑇loc⋅subscript𝑇clicksubscript𝑁clicksubscript𝑁frameT_{\mathrm{exam}}\cdot\left(L/300\right)+\left(T_{\mathrm{loc}}+T_{\mathrm{click}}\cdot N_{\mathrm{click}}\right)\cdot N_{\mathrm{frame}} in online mode, where L𝐿L is the total frame number in the video, Nframe=1,…,8subscript𝑁frame1…8N_{\mathrm{frame}}=1,\ldots,8 is the number of frames annotated (i.e., the number of interactive rounds), and Nclick=3subscript𝑁click3N_{\mathrm{click}}=3 is the number of clicks per frame.222We note that this estimation does not account for the model’s tracking FPS. The intuition is that human annotators can only examine the results at a lower speed, and therefore the model’s tracking time is covered by Texamsubscript𝑇examT_{\mathrm{exam}}.
items.zh: 一个对象的标注时间为：离线模式下为(T_exam·(L/300)+T_loc+T_click·N_click)·N_frame，在线模式下为T_exam·(L/300)+(T_loc+T_click·N_click)·N_frame，其中L是视频的总帧数，N_frame=1,…,8是标注的帧数（即交互轮数），N_click=3是每帧的点击次数。我们注意到，此估计未考虑模型的跟踪FPS。直觉上，人类标注者只能以较低速度检查结果，因此模型的跟踪时间被T_exam覆盖。

items.en: We show per-dataset results of SAM 2 and the two baselines (SAM+XMem++ and SAM+Cutie, see their details below) for interactive offline and online evaluation in Fig. 12 and Fig. 13. SAM 2 outperforms both baselines with a notable margin on all datasets and settings.
items.zh: 我们在图12和图13中展示了SAM 2和两个基线（SAM+XMem++和SAM+Cutie，详见下文）在交互式离线和在线评估中的每个数据集结果。SAM 2在所有数据集和设置上均以显著优势优于两个基线。

items.capEn: Figure 12: Zero-shot performance of SAM 2 vs baselines (SAM+XMem++ and SAM+Cutie) under interactive offline evaluation with different numbers of interacted frames, using 3 clicks per interacted frame. See §E.1.2 for details.
items.capZh: 图12：SAM 2与基线（SAM+XMem++和SAM+Cutie）在交互式离线评估中的零样本性能，使用不同数量的交互帧，每交互帧3次点击。详见§E.1.2。

items.capEn: Figure 13: Zero-shot performance of SAM 2 vs baselines (SAM+XMem++ and SAM+Cutie) under interactive online evaluation with different numbers of interacted frames, using 3 clicks per interacted frame. See §E.1.2 for details.
items.capZh: 图13：SAM 2与基线（SAM+XMem++和SAM+Cutie）在交互式在线评估中的零样本性能，使用不同数量的交互帧，每交互帧3次点击。详见§E.1.2。

items.en: In §6.1.2, we also compare with previous video tracking methods under the semi-supervised VOS setting (Pont-Tuset et al., 2017), where prompts (which can be foreground/background clicks, bounding boxes, or ground-truth object masks) are provided only on the first frame of the video. When using click prompts, we interactively sample either 1, 3 or 5 clicks on the first video frame, and then track the object based on these clicks. Following the click-based evaluation in prior work (Kirillov et al., 2023; Sofiiuk et al., 2022), the initial click is placed on the object center and subsequent clicks are obtained from the center of the error region.
items.zh: 在§6.1.2中，我们还将与半监督VOS设置下的先前视频跟踪方法进行比较（Pont-Tuset等人，2017），该设置仅在视频的第一帧提供提示（可以是前景/背景点击、边界框或真实对象掩码）。当使用点击提示时，我们在第一帧上交互式采样1、3或5次点击，然后基于这些点击跟踪对象。遵循先前工作中基于点击的评估（Kirillov等人，2023；Sofiiuk等人，2022），初始点击放置在对象中心，后续点击来自误差区域中心。

items.en: Similar to the interactive setting, here we also use SAM+XMem++ and SAM+Cutie as two baselines. For click or box prompts, SAM is first used to handle click or bounding box inputs, and its output mask is then used as input to XMem++ or Cutie. For mask prompts, the ground-truth object masks on the first frame are directly used as input to XMem++ and Cutie – this is the standard semi-supervised VOS setting and evaluates XMem++ and Cutie without using SAM.
items.zh: 与交互式设置类似，这里我们也使用SAM+XMem++和SAM+Cutie作为两个基线。对于点击或框提示，首先使用SAM处理点击或边界框输入，然后将SAM的输出掩码作为XMem++或Cutie的输入。对于掩码提示，第一帧上的真实对象掩码直接用作XMem++和Cutie的输入——这是标准的半监督VOS设置，评估时不使用SAM。

items.en: In this setting, we evaluate on all 17 zero-shot video datasets in §E.1.1. If a dataset does not follow the standard VOS format, we preprocess it into a format similar to MOSE (Ding et al., 2023). During processing, we ensure that all objects in each video have a valid non-empty segmentation mask on the first frame to be compatible with semi-supervised VOS evaluation. In case an object doesn’t appear in the first frame, we create a separate video for it starting from the first frame where the object appears.
items.zh: 在此设置中，我们在§E.1.1中的所有17个零样本视频数据集上进行评估。如果数据集不遵循标准VOS格式，我们将其预处理为类似于MOSE的格式（Ding等人，2023）。在处理过程中，我们确保每个视频中的所有对象在第一帧上都有有效的非空分割掩码，以兼容半监督VOS评估。如果某个对象未出现在第一帧中，我们为该对象创建一个单独的视频，从该对象出现的第一帧开始。

items.en: We report the standard 𝒥&ℱ𝒥ℱ\mathcal{J}\&\mathcal{F} metric (Pont-Tuset et al., 2017) for this evaluation. If a dataset provides an official evaluation toolkit, we use it for evaluation (on the VOST dataset, we report the 𝒥𝒥\mathcal{J} metric instead, following its official protocol (Tokmakov et al., 2022)). The results are shown in Table 4, where SAM 2 performs better than both baselines on the majority of the 17 datasets across different types of prompts.
items.zh: 我们报告此评估的标准𝒥&ℱ指标（Pont-Tuset等人，2017）。如果数据集提供官方评估工具包，我们使用它进行评估（在VOST数据集上，我们报告𝒥指标，遵循其官方协议（Tokmakov等人，2022））。结果如表4所示，SAM 2在17个数据集中的大多数上，在不同类型的提示下均优于两个基线。

items.en: We show per-dataset results of SAM 2 and the two baselines (SAM+XMem++ and SAM+Cutie, see their details below) for semi-supervised VOS evaluation in Fig. 14. SAM 2 outperforms both baselines on the majority of these datasets across different types of prompts.
items.zh: 我们在图14中展示了SAM 2和两个基线（SAM+XMem++和SAM+Cutie，详见下文）在半监督VOS评估中的每个数据集结果。SAM 2在这些数据集中的大多数上，在不同类型的提示下均优于两个基线。

items.capEn: Figure 14: Zero-shot performance on 17 video datasets of SAM 2 vs two baselines (SAM+XMem++ and SAM+Cutie) under semi-supervised VOS evaluation using different prompts (1, 3 or 5 clicks, bounding boxes, or ground-truth masks on the first video frame), with the averaged performance across datasets for each type of prompt shown in Table 4 in the main text. See §E.1.3 for details.
items.capZh: 图14：SAM 2与两个基线（SAM+XMem++和SAM+Cutie）在17个视频数据集上的零样本性能，使用不同提示（第一帧上的1、3或5次点击、边界框或真实掩码），每种提示类型在数据集上的平均性能如正文表4所示。详见§E.1.3。

items.en: We adopt SAM+XMem++ and SAM+Cutie as two baselines for promptable video segmentation, where the click (or box) prompts are first processed by SAM to obtain an object mask, and then XMem++ / Cutie models track this SAM mask across the video to obtain the final masklet. In these two baselines, SAM can be used to provide both an initial object mask on the first frame, or to correct an existing object mask output by XMem++ or Cutie. This is used for subsequent interacted frames during interactive offline and online evaluation, where new positive and negative clicks are provided as corrections over an existing mask.
items.zh: 我们采用SAM+XMem++和SAM+Cutie作为可提示视频分割的两个基线，其中点击（或框）提示首先由SAM处理以获得对象掩码，然后XMem++/Cutie模型在视频中跟踪该SAM掩码以获得最终掩码片段。在这两个基线中，SAM可用于提供第一帧上的初始对象掩码，或修正XMem++或Cutie输出的现有对象掩码。这用于交互式离线和在线评估中的后续交互帧，其中提供新的正负点击作为对现有掩码的修正。

items.en: When using SAM to apply a correction over an existing mask prediction in a given frame, we follow the strategy in EVA-VOS (Delatolas et al., 2024) to first initialize SAM with the XMem++ or Cutie output mask before incorporating the new correction clicks. Specifically, we first reconstruct the XMem++ or Cutie output mask in SAM by sampling clicks from them and feeding them as inputs to SAM until the reconstructed mask in SAM reaches IoU>0.8IoU0.8\mathrm{IoU}>0.8 with the XMem++ or Cutie output mask. Then, to incorporate new positive and negative clicks for correction, we concatenate these additional correction clicks with the initial clicks sampled during mask construction, and feed the joint concatenated list as input into SAM to obtain the final corrected masks. We find that this strategy works better than several alternatives (such as feeding the XMem++ or Cutie output mask as a mask prompt together with new correction clicks into SAM, or taking only the correction clicks as inputs to SAM while ignoring the XMem++ or Cutie output mask).
items.zh: 当使用SAM对给定帧中的现有掩码预测进行修正时，我们遵循EVA-VOS中的策略（Delatolas等人，2024），首先用XMem++或Cutie输出掩码初始化SAM，然后合并新的修正点击。具体来说，我们首先通过从XMem++或Cutie输出掩码中采样点击并将其作为输入馈送到SAM来重建SAM中的掩码，直到SAM中的重建掩码与XMem++或Cutie输出掩码的IoU>0.8。然后，为了合并新的正负点击进行修正，我们将这些额外的修正点击与掩码构建期间采样的初始点击连接起来，并将连接后的列表作为输入馈送到SAM，以获得最终的修正掩码。我们发现，此策略优于几种替代方案（例如，将XMem++或Cutie输出掩码作为掩码提示与新的修正点击一起馈送到SAM，或仅将修正点击作为SAM的输入而忽略XMem++或Cutie输出掩码）。

items.en: We also evaluate on the DAVIS interactive benchmark (Caelles et al., 2018), which resembles our interactive offline evaluation in §6.1.1, where in each round of interaction, the evaluation server would provide new annotations on frames with the worst segmentation performance. The official DAVIS eval toolkit provides scribble prompts during interactions, while other work such as CiVOS (Vujasinović et al., 2022) has also extended this to cover click prompts.
items.zh: 我们还在DAVIS交互式基准上进行评估（Caelles等人，2018），该基准类似于我们在§6.1.1中的交互式离线评估，在每轮交互中，评估服务器会在分割性能最差的帧上提供新的标注。官方DAVIS评估工具包在交互过程中提供涂鸦提示，而其他工作如CiVOS（Vujasinović等人，2022）已将其扩展到覆盖点击提示。

items.en: Here we follow CiVOS to use positive and negative clicks as input prompts and adopt the same strategy for click sampling. We report the 𝒥&ℱ𝒥ℱ\mathcal{J}\&\mathcal{F}@60s and AUC-𝒥&ℱ𝒥ℱ\mathcal{J}\&\mathcal{F} metrics on this benchmark as provided by its evaluator, and compare to two baselines: MiVOS (Cheng et al., 2021b), which directly uses the provided scribbles via a scribble-to-mask module (and is also extended to click prompts in Vujasinović et al. (2022)), and CiVOS, which samples click from the provided scribbles. The results are shown in Table 14, where SAM 2 (based on click inputs) outperforms both baselines under click inputs. We note that SAM 2 often tends to segment object parts (e.g. a person’s arm) on the first click while the DAVIS dataset mainly contains whole objects (e.g. an entire person), which could penalize SAM 2’s 𝒥𝒥\mathcal{J}&ℱℱ\mathcal{F} performance on this benchmark. We verified this by observing better accuracy (0.86 AUC-𝒥𝒥\mathcal{J}&ℱℱ\mathcal{F} and 0.89 𝒥𝒥\mathcal{J}&ℱℱ\mathcal{F}@60s with click input) for an earlier model trained on fewer part annotations.
items.zh: 这里我们遵循CiVOS使用正负点击作为输入提示，并采用相同的点击采样策略。我们报告该基准上的𝒥&ℱ@60s和AUC-𝒥&ℱ指标，并与两个基线进行比较：MiVOS（Cheng等人，2021b），它通过涂鸦到掩码模块直接使用提供的涂鸦（并在Vujasinović等人（2022）中扩展到点击提示）；以及CiVOS，它从提供的涂鸦中采样点击。结果如表14所示，SAM 2（基于点击输入）在点击输入下优于两个基线。我们注意到，SAM 2在第一次点击时往往倾向于分割对象部分（例如人的手臂），而DAVIS数据集主要包含完整对象（例如整个人），这可能会降低SAM 2在该基准上的𝒥&ℱ性能。我们通过观察一个在较少部分标注上训练的早期模型具有更好的准确率（点击输入下AUC-𝒥&ℱ为0.86，𝒥&ℱ@60s为0.89）验证了这一点。

items.en: For the interactive segmentation task, we evaluated SAM 2 on a comprehensive suite of 37 datasets. This suite includes the 23 datasets previously used by SAM for zero-shot evaluation. For completeness, we list the 23 datasets: LVIS (Gupta et al., 2019), ADE20K (Zhou et al., 2019), Hypersim (Roberts et al., 2021), Cityscapes (Cordts et al., 2016), BBBC038v1 (Caicedo et al., 2019), DOORS (Pugliatti & Topputo, 2022), DRAM (Cohen et al., 2022), EgoHOS (Zhang et al., 2022), GTEA (Fathi et al., 2011; Li et al., 2015), iShape (Yang et al., 2021a), NDD20 (Trotter et al., 2020), NDISPark (Ciampi et al., 2021, 2022), OVIS (Qi et al., 2022), PPDLS (Minervini et al., 2016), Plittersdorf (Haucke et al., 2022), STREETS (Snyder & Do, 2019), TimberSeg (Fortin et al., 2022), TrashCan (Hong et al., 2020), VISOR (Darkhalil et al., 2022; Damen et al., 2022), WoodScape (Yogamani et al., 2019), PIDRay (Wang et al., 2021a), ZeroWaste-f (Bashkirova et al., 2022), and IBD (Chen et al., 2022). For more detailed information about these datasets, we refer the reader to Kirillov et al. (2023). In addition to these 23 datasets, we evaluated on frames sampled from 14 video datasets to assess SAM 2’s performance on images from the video domain. The video datasets used are listed as follows: Lindenthal Camera Traps (LCT) (Haucke & Steinhage, 2021), VOST (Tokmakov et al., 2022), LV-VIS (Wang et al., 2023), FBMS (Brox et al., 2010), Virtual KITTI 2 (Cabon et al., 2020), Corsican Fire Database (CFD) (Toulouse et al., 2017), VIPSeg (Miao et al., 2022), Drosophila Heart OCM (DH OCM) (Fishman et al., 2023), EndoVis 2018 (Allan et al., 2020), ESD (Huang et al., 2023), UVO (Wang et al., 2021b), Ego-Exo4d (Grauman et al., 2023), LVOSv2 (Hong et al., 2024), and HT1080WT (Gómez-de Mariscal et al., 2021). Table 16 has a more detailed description of these datasets. (Some of these datasets are obtained from the same data source as the zero-shot video datasets in §E.1.1.)
items.zh: 对于交互式分割任务，我们在包含37个数据集的综合套件上评估了SAM 2。该套件包括SAM先前用于零样本评估的23个数据集。为完整起见，我们列出这23个数据集：LVIS (Gupta et al., 2019)、ADE20K (Zhou et al., 2019)、Hypersim (Roberts et al., 2021)、Cityscapes (Cordts et al., 2016)、BBBC038v1 (Caicedo et al., 2019)、DOORS (Pugliatti & Topputo, 2022)、DRAM (Cohen et al., 2022)、EgoHOS (Zhang et al., 2022)、GTEA (Fathi et al., 2011; Li et al., 2015)、iShape (Yang et al., 2021a)、NDD20 (Trotter et al., 2020)、NDISPark (Ciampi et al., 2021, 2022)、OVIS (Qi et al., 2022)、PPDLS (Minervini et al., 2016)、Plittersdorf (Haucke et al., 2022)、STREETS (Snyder & Do, 2019)、TimberSeg (Fortin et al., 2022)、TrashCan (Hong et al., 2020)、VISOR (Darkhalil et al., 2022; Damen et al., 2022)、WoodScape (Yogamani et al., 2019)、PIDRay (Wang et al., 2021a)、ZeroWaste-f (Bashkirova et al., 2022) 和 IBD (Chen et al., 2022)。关于这些数据集的更多详细信息，请参考 Kirillov et al. (2023)。除了这23个数据集，我们还在从14个视频数据集中采样的帧上进行了评估，以评估SAM 2在视频域图像上的性能。使用的视频数据集如下：Lindenthal Camera Traps (LCT) (Haucke & Steinhage, 2021)、VOST (Tokmakov et al., 2022)、LV-VIS (Wang et al., 2023)、FBMS (Brox et al., 2010)、Virtual KITTI 2 (Cabon et al., 2020)、Corsican Fire Database (CFD) (Toulouse et al., 2017)、VIPSeg (Miao et al., 2022)、Drosophila Heart OCM (DH OCM) (Fishman et al., 2023)、EndoVis 2018 (Allan et al., 2020)、ESD (Huang et al., 2023)、UVO (Wang et al., 2021b)、Ego-Exo4d (Grauman et al., 2023)、LVOSv2 (Hong et al., 2024) 和 HT1080WT (Gómez-de Mariscal et al., 2021)。表16对这些数据集有更详细的描述。（其中一些数据集与§E.1.1中零样本视频数据集的数据来源相同。）

items.en: In this section, we include a more detailed version of the experiments in §6.2. We compare SAM 2 to SAM and HQ-SAM with different model sizes in Table 15. The main metrics we use for evaluation are the 1- and 5-click mIoU and we categorize the results by the dataset domain.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们提供了§6.2中实验的更详细版本。我们在表15中将SAM 2与不同模型大小的SAM和HQ-SAM进行了比较。我们用于评估的主要指标是1次点击和5次点击的mIoU，并按数据集领域对结果进行分类。

items.en: The last two rows in Table 15 illustrate the benefits of training with our mix of image and video data, which boosts the average accuracy to 61.4% across the 23 datasets with the Hirea-B+ image encoder. Additionally, we observe substantial improvements on the video benchmarks of SA-23 as well as the 14 newly introduced video datasets. We note that we do not scale beyond Hiera-L, but expect better performance for a larger model.
items.zh: 表15的最后两行展示了使用我们的图像和视频数据混合进行训练的好处，这使得使用Hiera-B+图像编码器在23个数据集上的平均准确率提升至61.4%。此外，我们观察到在SA-23的视频基准测试以及14个新引入的视频数据集上也有显著改进。我们注意到，我们没有扩展到Hiera-L以上，但预计更大的模型会有更好的性能。

items.capEn: Figure 15: Zero-shot performance of SAM 2 vs SAM on a suite of 37 datasets. The figure shows the center 1 click mIoU delta between SAM 2 and SAM. Datasets derived from video distribution are highlighted in red, while those from image distribution are highlighted in blue.
items.capZh: 图15：SAM 2与SAM在37个数据集套件上的零样本性能。该图显示了SAM 2与SAM之间的中心1次点击mIoU差值。来自视频分布的数据集以红色突出显示，来自图像分布的数据集以蓝色突出显示。

items.en: A breakdown of the accuracy across datasets is presented in Fig. 16, where the per-dataset delta in 1-click mIoU relative to SAM is color-coded to indicate the data type (image or video). Notably, SAM 2 (Hiera-B+) surpasses SAM on 29 datasets333We note that OVIS is not strictly zero-shot for SAM 2 since its videos are used in MOSE (part of our training data mix). by up to 53.9 mIoU, despite using a smaller Hiera-B+ image encoder.
items.zh: 图16展示了各数据集准确率的详细分解，其中每个数据集相对于SAM的1次点击mIoU差值用颜色编码表示数据类型（图像或视频）。值得注意的是，尽管使用了更小的Hiera-B+图像编码器，SAM 2（Hiera-B+）在29个数据集上超越了SAM，最高提升达53.9 mIoU。

items.capEn: Figure 17: Examples from SAM 2 zero-shot video benchmark suite.
items.capZh: 图17：SAM 2零样本视频基准测试套件示例。

full.sec: Appendix F Details on comparison to state-of-the-art in semi-supervised VOS
full.secZh: 附录F 与半监督VOS最新技术比较的细节

items.en: We provide additional details on the comparison to the previous state-of-the-art in semi-supervised VOS (§7). We include results from SAM 2 trained only on SA-1B, SA-V and Internal data, for different encoder sizes.
items.zh: 我们提供了与半监督VOS（§7）先前最先进方法的比较的更多细节。我们包含了仅使用SA-1B、SA-V和内部数据训练的SAM 2在不同编码器大小下的结果。

items.en: Qualitative comparison: In Fig. 16, we show a comparison between our baseline (Cutie-base+, top row) and our model (SAM 2, bottom row) when prompted with a mask in the first frame. While the mask prompt in the first frame only covers the shirt of the person, the masklet predicted by the baseline wrongfully propagates to the whole person. Our model, however, is able to restrict the masklet to the target object.
items.zh: 定性比较：在图16中，我们展示了当在第一帧中给定掩码提示时，我们的基线（Cutie-base+，上行）和我们的模型（SAM 2，下行）之间的比较。虽然第一帧中的掩码提示仅覆盖了人物的衬衫，但基线预测的掩码错误地传播到了整个人物。然而，我们的模型能够将掩码限制在目标对象上。

items.en: Quantitative comparison: In Table 17, we compare the performance of our model to previous approaches on additional semi-supervised VOS metrics. SAM 2 outperforms prior work on all evaluated benchmarks, in all metrics. Note that unlike these previous approaches, SAM 2 is not specialized in the semi-supervised VOS task but is capable of more general promptable segmentation. SAM 2 is also not restricted to a specific set of object classes. The performance of our model on the SA-V benchmark (Table LABEL:tab:appendix_sa-v) demonstrates its capability to segment anything in a video.
items.zh: 定量比较：在表17中，我们将我们的模型与先前方法在额外的半监督VOS指标上进行了比较。SAM 2在所有评估基准的所有指标上均优于先前工作。请注意，与这些先前方法不同，SAM 2并非专门针对半监督VOS任务，而是能够进行更通用的可提示分割。SAM 2也不限于特定的对象类别集。我们的模型在SA-V基准测试（表LABEL:tab:appendix_sa-v）上的性能证明了其在视频中分割任何物体的能力。

full.sec: G.2 Dataset card for SA-V dataset

items.en: For what purpose was the dataset created? Was there a specific task in mind? Was there a specific gap that needed to be filled? Please provide a description. The dataset was designed for the PVS task. The contributions of our dataset to the vision community are: (1) The dataset, composed of 50.9K videos and 642.6K masklets, is the largest video segmentation dataset publicly available today (see 5.2 for comparisons to current VOS datasets) (2) The dataset is available under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Public License at https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video/, (3) The data is a more geographically diverse, publicly available, video segmentation dataset than its predecessors.
items.zh: 创建数据集的目的是什么？是否有特定的任务？是否有需要填补的特定空白？请提供描述。该数据集是为PVS任务设计的。我们的数据集对视觉社区的贡献如下：（1）该数据集包含50.9K个视频和642.6K个掩码，是当前公开可用的最大视频分割数据集（参见5.2与当前VOS数据集的比较）；（2）该数据集在Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Public License下提供，网址为https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video/；（3）该数据是比其前身地理多样性更广、公开可用的视频分割数据集。

items.en: Who created the dataset (e.g., which team, research group) and on behalf of which entity (e.g., company, institution, organization)? The dataset was created by Meta FAIR. The underlying videos were collected via a contracted third party company.
items.zh: 谁创建了数据集（例如，哪个团队、研究组）以及代表哪个实体（例如，公司、机构、组织）？该数据集由Meta FAIR创建。基础视频通过签约的第三方公司收集。

items.en: Who funded the creation of the dataset? The dataset was funded by Meta FAIR.
items.zh: 谁资助了数据集的创建？该数据集由Meta FAIR资助。

items.en: What do the instances that comprise the dataset represent (e.g., documents, photos, people, countries)? Are there multiple types of instances (e.g., movies, users, and ratings; people and interactions between them; nodes and edges)? Please provide a description. All of the instances in the dataset are videos. Subject matter diversity was encouraged and no specific themes were applied during video collection. Common themes of the video include: locations, objects, scenes. All the videos are distinct, however there are some sets of videos that were taken of the same subject matter.
items.zh: 构成数据集的实例代表什么（例如，文档、照片、人物、国家）？是否有多种类型的实例（例如，电影、用户和评分；人物及其交互；节点和边）？请提供描述。数据集中的所有实例都是视频。鼓励主题多样性，在视频收集过程中没有应用特定主题。视频的常见主题包括：地点、物体、场景。所有视频都是不同的，但有一些视频集拍摄的是相同主题。

items.en: How many instances are there in total (of each type, if appropriate)? There are 50.9K videos.
items.zh: 总共有多少个实例（如果合适，每种类型有多少个）？有50.9K个视频。

items.en: Does the dataset contain all possible instances or is it a sample (not necessarily random) of instances from a larger set? If the dataset is a sample, then what is the larger set? Is the sample representative of the larger set (e.g., geographic coverage)? If so, please describe how this representativeness was validated/verified. If it is not representative of the larger set, please describe why not (e.g., to cover a more diverse range of instances, because instances were withheld or unavailable). While the dataset contains all possible instances, reviewers were advised to refuse to annotate content containing explicit imagery.
items.zh: 数据集是否包含所有可能的实例，还是来自更大集合的样本（不一定是随机样本）？如果数据集是样本，那么更大的集合是什么？样本是否代表更大的集合（例如，地理覆盖）？如果是，请描述如何验证/确认这种代表性。如果它不代表更大的集合，请说明原因（例如，为了覆盖更多样化的实例范围，因为实例被保留或不可用）。虽然数据集包含所有可能的实例，但评审员被建议拒绝包含露骨图像的注释内容。

items.en: What data does each instance consist of? “Raw” data (e.g., unprocessed text or images) or features? In either case, please provide a description. Each instance is a video.
items.zh: 每个实例包含什么数据？“原始”数据（例如，未处理的文本或图像）还是特征？无论哪种情况，请提供描述。每个实例是一个视频。

items.en: Is there a label or target associated with each instance? If so, please provide a description. Each video is annotated with masklets that track objects throughout the video. There are no categories or text associated with the masklets. The data was annotated at 6 FPS. There are an average of 3.8 manual masklets, and 8.9 auto masklets per video, and there are 642.6K masklets in total.
items.zh: 每个实例是否有标签或目标？如果有，请提供描述。每个视频都标注了跟踪视频中物体的掩码。掩码没有关联的类别或文本。数据以6 FPS进行标注。每个视频平均有3.8个手动掩码和8.9个自动掩码，总共642.6K个掩码。

items.en: Is any information missing from individual instances? If so, please provide a description, explaining why this information is missing (e.g., because it was unavailable). This does not include intentionally removed information, but might include, e.g., redacted text. No.
items.zh: 单个实例是否缺少任何信息？如果是，请提供描述，解释为什么缺少这些信息（例如，因为不可用）。这不包括故意删除的信息，但可能包括例如编辑过的文本。没有。

items.en: Are relationships between individual instances made explicit (e.g., users’ movie ratings, social network links)? If so, please describe how these relationships are made explicit. No.
items.zh: 单个实例之间的关系是否明确（例如，用户的电影评分、社交网络链接）？如果是，请描述这些关系是如何明确的。没有。

items.en: Are there any errors, sources of noise, or redundancies in the dataset? If so, please provide a description. For manual masklets, human errors may exist; for example, annotators may miss a frame to check or fix when needed. For auto masklets, as SAM 2 is used to generates them, model errors such as inconsistencies in the masklets may exist.
items.zh: 数据集中是否存在任何错误、噪声源或冗余？如果是，请提供描述。对于手动掩码，可能存在人为错误；例如，标注者可能错过需要检查或修复的帧。对于自动掩码，由于使用SAM 2生成，可能存在模型错误，例如掩码中的不一致性。

items.en: Is the dataset self-contained, or does it link to or otherwise rely on external resources (e.g., websites, tweets, other datasets)? If it links to or relies on external resources, a) are there guarantees that they will exist, and remain constant, over time; b) are there official archival versions of the complete dataset (e.g., including the external resources as they existed at the time the dataset was created); c) are there any restrictions (e.g., licenses, fees) associated with any of the external resources that might apply to a dataset consumer? Please provide descriptions of all external resources and any restrictions associated with them, as well as links or other access points, as appropriate. The dataset is self contained.
items.zh: 数据集是自包含的，还是链接到或依赖外部资源（例如，网站、推文、其他数据集）？如果它链接到或依赖外部资源，a) 是否有保证这些资源会长期存在并保持不变；b) 是否有完整数据集的官方存档版本（例如，包括数据集创建时存在的外部资源）；c) 与任何外部资源相关的限制（例如，许可证、费用）是否可能适用于数据集消费者？请提供所有外部资源及其相关限制的描述，以及适当的链接或其他访问点。数据集是自包含的。

items.en: Does the dataset contain data that might be considered confidential (e.g., data that is protected by legal privilege or by doctor-patient confidentiality, data that includes the content of individuals’ non-public communications)? If so, please provide a description. No.
items.zh: 数据集是否包含可能被视为机密的数据（例如，受法律特权或医患保密保护的数据，包含个人非公开通信内容的数据）？如果是，请提供描述。否。

items.en: Does the dataset contain data that, if viewed directly, might be offensive, insulting, threatening, or might otherwise cause anxiety? If so, please describe why. We have three safety measures to prevent objectionable content: (1) The video collecting crowdworkers were provided instructions to not record videos that might contain objectionable content (e.g., graphic, nudity, or inappropriate content). (2) The expert annotators who annotated the videos were provided instructions to flag and reject videos if objectionable content was present. (3) reports about video(s) in the dataset can be submitted to segment-anything@meta.com.
items.zh: 数据集是否包含如果直接查看可能会令人反感、侮辱、威胁或引起焦虑的数据？如果是，请说明原因。我们有三项安全措施来防止令人反感的内容：（1）视频收集众包工作者被指示不要录制可能包含令人反感内容的视频（例如，暴力、裸露或不适当的内容）。（2）注释视频的专家注释者被指示在存在令人反感内容时标记并拒绝视频。（3）关于数据集中视频的报告可以提交至segment-anything@meta.com。

items.en: Does the dataset identify any subpopulations (e.g., by age, gender)? If so, please describe how these subpopulations are identified and provide a description of their respective distributions within the dataset. The dataset does not identify any subpopulations of the people in the videos. The demographics of the crowdworkers who collected the videos in the dataset are presented in 5.2.
items.zh: 数据集是否识别任何子群体（例如，按年龄、性别）？如果是，请描述这些子群体是如何被识别的，并提供它们在数据集中的分布描述。数据集不识别视频中人物的任何子群体。收集数据集中视频的众包工作者的人口统计数据在5.2节中呈现。

items.en: Is it possible to identify individuals (i.e, one or more natural persons), either directly or indirectly (i.e., in combination with other data) from the dataset? If so, please describe how. Videos were subjected to a face blurring model. Reports about videos in the dataset can be submitted to segment-anything@meta.com.
items.zh: 是否可能从数据集中直接或间接（即与其他数据结合）识别个人（即一个或多个自然人）？如果是，请描述如何识别。视频经过了面部模糊模型处理。关于数据集中视频的报告可以提交至segment-anything@meta.com。

items.en: Does the dataset contain data that might be considered sensitive in any way (e.g., data that reveals race or ethnic origins, sexual orientations, religious beliefs, political opinions or union memberships, or locations; financial or health data; biometric or genetic data; forms of government identification, such as social security numbers; criminal history)? If so, please provide a description. The dataset is not focused on data that may be considered sensitive. Reports about videos in the dataset can be submitted to segment-anything@meta.com.
items.zh: 数据集是否包含可能以任何方式被视为敏感的数据（例如，揭示种族或民族起源、性取向、宗教信仰、政治观点或工会成员身份、位置的数据；财务或健康数据；生物识别或基因数据；政府身份证明形式，如社会安全号码；犯罪历史）？如果是，请提供描述。数据集不关注可能被视为敏感的数据。关于数据集中视频的报告可以提交至segment-anything@meta.com。

items.en: How was the data associated with each instance acquired? Was the data directly observable (e.g., raw text, movie ratings), reported by subjects (e.g., survey responses), or indirectly inferred/derived from other data (e.g., part-of-speech tags, model-based guesses for age or language)? If the data was reported by subjects or indirectly inferred/derived from other data, was the data validated/verified? If so, please describe how. The released masklets associated with each video were collected using two methods. (1) SAM 2 assisted manual annotation (2) automatically generated by SAM 2 and verified by annotators.
items.zh: 每个实例关联的数据是如何获取的？数据是直接可观察的（例如，原始文本、电影评分），由受试者报告（例如，调查回复），还是从其他数据中间接推断/衍生（例如，词性标签、基于模型的年龄或语言猜测）？如果数据是由受试者报告或从其他数据中间接推断/衍生，数据是否经过验证/确认？如果是，请描述如何验证。每个视频关联的已发布masklet通过两种方法收集：（1）SAM 2辅助手动注释；（2）由SAM 2自动生成并由注释者验证。

items.en: What mechanisms or procedures were used to collect the data (e.g., hardware apparatuses or sensors, manual human curation, software programs, software APIs)? How were these mechanisms or procedures validated? The videos in the dataset were collected via a contracted third-party vendor. They are videos taken by crowdworkers with unknown equipment.
items.zh: 使用了哪些机制或程序来收集数据（例如，硬件设备或传感器、手动人工筛选、软件程序、软件API）？这些机制或程序是如何验证的？数据集中的视频通过签约的第三方供应商收集。它们是由众包工作者使用未知设备拍摄的视频。

items.en: If the dataset is a sample from a larger set, what was the sampling strategy (e.g., deterministic, probabilistic with specific sampling probabilities)? N/A.
items.zh: 如果数据集是更大集合的样本，采样策略是什么（例如，确定性、具有特定采样概率的概率性）？不适用。

items.en: Who was involved in the data collection process (e.g., students, crowdworkers, contractors) and how were they compensated (e.g., how much were crowdworkers paid)? (1) The videos in the dataset were collected via a contracted third-party vendor. They are videos taken by crowdworkers who were compensated with an hourly wage set by the vendor. (2) The manually collected masklets in the dataset were collected by annotators via another third-party vendor. Annotators were compensated with an hourly wage set by the vendor.
items.zh: 谁参与了数据收集过程（例如，学生、众包工作者、承包商），他们是如何获得报酬的（例如，众包工作者获得了多少报酬）？（1）数据集中的视频通过签约的第三方供应商收集。它们是由众包工作者拍摄的，这些工作者按供应商设定的小时工资获得报酬。（2）数据集中的手动收集masklet由注释者通过另一家第三方供应商收集。注释者按供应商设定的小时工资获得报酬。

items.en: Over what timeframe was the data collected? Does this timeframe match the creation timeframe of the data associated with the instances (e.g., recent crawl of old news articles)? If not, please describe the timeframe in which the data associated with the instances was created. The videos were filmed between November 2023 and March 2024. The masklet annotations were collected between April 2024 and July 2024.
items.zh: 数据收集的时间范围是什么？这个时间范围是否与实例关联数据的创建时间范围相匹配（例如，最近爬取的旧新闻文章）？如果不匹配，请描述实例关联数据的创建时间范围。视频拍摄于2023年11月至2024年3月之间。masklet注释收集于2024年4月至2024年7月之间。

items.en: Were any ethical review processes conducted (e.g., by an institutional review board)? If so, please provide a description of these review processes, including the outcomes, as well as a link or other access point to any supporting documentation. If the dataset does not relate to people, you may skip the remaining questions in this section. The project underwent an internal review process.
items.zh: 是否进行了任何伦理审查过程（例如，由机构审查委员会）？如果是，请提供这些审查过程的描述，包括结果，以及任何支持文档的链接或其他访问点。如果数据集不涉及人员，您可以跳过本节中的其余问题。该项目经历了内部审查过程。

items.en: Did you collect the data from the individuals in question directly, or obtain it via third parties or other sources (e.g. websites)? We contracted with third-party vendors to collect the videos and to generate or review annotations.
items.zh: 您是直接从相关个人收集数据，还是通过第三方或其他来源（例如，网站）获取？我们与第三方供应商签约以收集视频并生成或审查注释。

items.en: Were the individuals in question notified about the data collection? If so, please describe (or show with screenshots or other information) how notice was provided, and provide a link or other access point to, or otherwise reproduce, the exact language of the notification itself. The videos were collected by crowdworkers via a contracted third-party vendor. The crowdworkers agreed to consent forms.
items.zh: 相关个人是否被告知数据收集？如果是，请描述（或通过截图或其他信息展示）通知是如何提供的，并提供通知确切语言的链接或其他访问点，或复制该语言。视频由众包工作者通过签约的第三方供应商收集。众包工作者同意了同意书。

items.en: Did the individuals in question consent to the collection and use of their data? If so, please describe (or show with screenshots or other information) how consent was requested and provided, and provide a link or other access point to, or otherwise reproduce, the exact language to which the individuals consented. The videos were collected via a contracted third-party who provided appropriate representations regarding the collection of any notices and consents as required from individuals.
items.zh: 相关个人是否同意收集和使用他们的数据？如果是，请描述（或通过截图或其他信息展示）同意是如何请求和提供的，并提供个人同意确切语言的链接或其他访问点，或复制该语言。视频通过签约的第三方收集，该第三方就收集个人所需的任何通知和同意提供了适当的陈述。

items.en: If consent was obtained, were the consenting individuals provided with a mechanism to revoke their consent in the future or for certain uses? If so, please provide a description, as well as a link or other access point to the mechanism (if appropriate). Pursuant to the contract, the contracted third-party collected consents and provided opportunity for consent revocation.
items.zh: 如果获得了同意，是否向同意者提供了未来或针对某些用途撤销同意的机制？如果是，请提供描述，以及该机制的链接或其他访问点（如果适用）。根据合同，签约的第三方收集了同意并提供了撤销同意的机会。

items.en: Has an analysis of the potential impact of the dataset and its use on data subjects (e.g., a data protection impact analysis) been conducted? If so, please provide a description of this analysis, including the outcomes, as well as a link or other access point to any supporting documentation. See detail in 6.1.3.
items.zh: 是否对数据集及其使用对数据主体的潜在影响进行了分析（例如，数据保护影响分析）？如果是，请提供此分析的描述，包括结果，以及任何支持文档的链接或其他访问点。详见6.1.3节。

items.en: Was any preprocessing / cleaning / labeling of the data done (e.g., discretization or bucketing, tokenization, part-of-speech tagging, SIFT feature extraction, removal of instances, processing of missing values)? If so, please provide a description. If not, you may skip the remaining questions in this section. The videos were re-sampled to 24 fps and converted to mp4 format.
items.zh: 是否对数据进行了任何预处理/清洗/标注（例如，离散化或分桶、分词、词性标注、SIFT特征提取、移除实例、处理缺失值）？如果是，请提供描述。如果没有，您可以跳过本节中的其余问题。视频被重新采样为24 fps并转换为mp4格式。

items.en: Was the “raw” data saved in addition to the preprocessed/cleaned/labeled data (e.g., to support unanticipated future uses)? If so, please provide a link or other access point to the “raw” data. No.
items.zh: 除了预处理/清洗/标注的数据外，是否还保存了“原始”数据（例如，以支持未预见的未来用途）？如果是，请提供“原始”数据的链接或其他访问点。否。

items.en: Has the dataset been used for any tasks already? If so, please provide a description. The dataset has been used to train and evaluate SAM 2.
items.zh: 数据集是否已经用于任何任务？如果是，请提供描述。该数据集已用于训练和评估SAM 2。

items.en: What (other) tasks could the dataset be used for? The data could be used for VOS, iVOS, or PVS tasks. If frames are sampled from the videos, the dataset can be used for the image segmentation task.
items.zh: 该数据集还能用于哪些（其他）任务？数据可用于VOS、iVOS或PVS任务。如果从视频中采样帧，数据集可用于图像分割任务。

items.en: Is there anything about the composition of the dataset or the way it was collected and preprocessed/cleaned/labeled that might impact future uses? For example, is there anything that a dataset consumer might need to know to avoid uses that could result in unfair treatment of individuals or groups (e.g., stereotyping, quality of service issues) or other risks or harms (e.g., legal risks, financial harms)? If so, please provide a description. Is there anything a dataset consumer could do to mitigate these risks or harms? We have an analysis of the geography and crowdworker demographic of our dataset in 5.2. While we believe our dataset to be more representative on these factors than most of the publicly existing datasets of its kind at this time, we acknowledge that we do not have parity across all geographic and demographic groups, and we encourage users of the dataset to be mindful of any potential biases models may learn using this dataset.
items.zh: 数据集的组成或其收集、预处理/清洗/标注方式是否可能影响未来使用？例如，是否存在数据集使用者需要了解的内容，以避免可能导致对个人或群体不公平对待（如刻板印象、服务质量问题）或其他风险或危害（如法律风险、财务损害）的使用？如果有，请提供描述。数据集使用者可以采取哪些措施来减轻这些风险或危害？我们在5.2节中对数据集的地理和众包工作者人口统计进行了分析。虽然我们相信我们的数据集在这些因素上比目前大多数同类公开数据集更具代表性，但我们承认并非所有地理和人口群体都达到均等，我们鼓励数据集使用者注意模型可能使用该数据集学习到的任何潜在偏见。

items.en: Are there tasks for which the dataset should not be used? If so, please provide a description. No. Full terms of use for the dataset can be found at https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video-downloads/.
items.zh: 是否存在不应使用该数据集的任务？如果有，请提供描述。没有。数据集的完整使用条款可在 https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video-downloads/ 找到。

items.en: Will the dataset be distributed to third parties outside of the entity (e.g., company, institution, organization) on behalf of which the dataset was created? If so, please provide a description. The dataset will be available under the permissive Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Public License.
items.zh: 数据集是否会分发给创建数据集所代表的实体（例如公司、机构、组织）之外的第三方？如果是，请提供描述。该数据集将在宽松的Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Public License下提供。

items.en: How will the dataset will be distributed (e.g., tarball on website, API, GitHub)? Does the dataset have a digital object identifier (DOI)? The dataset is available at https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video/.
items.zh: 数据集将如何分发（例如，网站上的tarball、API、GitHub）？数据集是否有数字对象标识符（DOI）？数据集可在 https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video/ 获取。

items.en: When will the dataset be distributed? The dataset will be distributed in July 2024.
items.zh: 数据集何时分发？数据集将于2024年7月分发。

items.en: Will the dataset be distributed under a copyright or other intellectual property (IP) license, and/or under applicable terms of use (ToU)? If so, please describe this license and/or ToU, and provide a link or other access point to, or otherwise reproduce, any relevant licensing terms or ToU, as well as any fees associated with these restrictions. Yes, the dataset will be available under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Public License. The license agreement and terms of use for the dataset can be found at https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video-downloads/. Users must agree to the terms of use before downloading or using the dataset.
items.zh: 数据集是否在版权或其他知识产权（IP）许可和/或适用使用条款（ToU）下分发？如果是，请描述此许可和/或ToU，并提供链接或其他访问点，或复制任何相关许可条款或ToU，以及这些限制相关的任何费用。是的，数据集将在Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Public License下提供。数据集的许可协议和使用条款可在 https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video-downloads/ 找到。用户在下载或使用数据集前必须同意使用条款。

items.en: Have any third parties imposed IP-based or other restrictions on the data associated with the instances? If so, please describe these restrictions, and provide a link or other access point to, or otherwise reproduce, any relevant licensing terms, as well as any fees associated with these restrictions. Full terms of use and restrictions on use of the SA-V dataset can be found at https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video-downloads/.
items.zh: 是否有任何第三方对与实例相关的数据施加了基于IP或其他限制？如果是，请描述这些限制，并提供链接或其他访问点，或复制任何相关许可条款，以及这些限制相关的任何费用。SA-V数据集的完整使用条款和使用限制可在 https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video-downloads/ 找到。

items.en: Do any export controls or other regulatory restrictions apply to the dataset or to individual instances? If so, please describe these restrictions, and provide a link or other access point to, or otherwise reproduce, any supporting documentation. The license and restrictions on use of the SA-V dataset can be found at https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video-downloads/.
items.zh: 是否有任何出口管制或其他监管限制适用于数据集或单个实例？如果是，请描述这些限制，并提供链接或其他访问点，或复制任何支持文档。SA-V数据集的许可和使用限制可在 https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video-downloads/ 找到。

items.en: Who will be supporting/hosting/maintaining the dataset? The dataset will be hosted at https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video/ and maintained by Meta FAIR.
items.zh: 谁将支持/托管/维护数据集？数据集将托管在 https://ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything-video/ ，并由Meta FAIR维护。

items.en: How can the owner/curator/manager of the dataset be contacted (e.g., email address)? Please email segment-anything@meta.com.
items.zh: 如何联系数据集的拥有者/管理者/维护者（例如电子邮件地址）？请发送邮件至 segment-anything@meta.com。

items.en: Is there an erratum? If so, please provide a link or other access point. No.
items.zh: 是否有勘误？如果有，请提供链接或其他访问点。没有。

items.en: Will the dataset be updated (e.g., to correct labeling errors, add new instances, delete instances)? If so, please describe how often, by whom, and how updates will be communicated to dataset consumers (e.g., mailing list, GitHub)? Updates may be made pursuant to inbound received at segment-anything@meta.com.
items.zh: 数据集是否会更新（例如，纠正标注错误、添加新实例、删除实例）？如果是，请描述更新频率、由谁更新以及如何将更新通知数据集使用者（例如邮件列表、GitHub）？可能会根据发送至 segment-anything@meta.com 的反馈进行更新。

items.en: If the dataset relates to people, are there applicable limits on the retention of the data associated with the instances (e.g., were the individuals in question told that their data would be retained for a fixed period of time and then deleted)? If so, please describe these limits and explain how they will be enforced. There are no limits on data retention.
items.zh: 如果数据集涉及人员，对与实例相关的数据保留是否有适用限制（例如，是否告知相关人员其数据将保留固定时间然后删除）？如果是，请描述这些限制并解释如何执行。没有数据保留限制。

items.en: Will older versions of the dataset continue to be supported/hosted/maintained? If so, please describe how. If not, please describe how its obsolescence will be communicated to dataset consumers. No. If updates are made to the dataset, previous versions will not continue to be hosted.
items.zh: 旧版本的数据集是否会继续得到支持/托管/维护？如果是，请描述如何支持。如果不是，请描述如何将废弃情况通知数据集使用者。不会。如果对数据集进行更新，旧版本将不再继续托管。

items.en: If others want to extend/augment/build on/contribute to the dataset, is there a mechanism for them to do so? If so, please provide a description. Will these contributions be validated/verified? If so, please describe how. If not, why not? Is there a process for communicating/distributing these contributions to dataset consumers? If so, please provide a description. We encourage further annotations for SA-V, but these will not be validated/verified or supported/hosted/maintained by Meta.
items.zh: 如果其他人想要扩展/增强/构建/贡献数据集，是否有机制允许他们这样做？如果是，请提供描述。这些贡献是否会得到验证/核实？如果是，请描述如何验证。如果不是，为什么？是否有将贡献传达/分发给数据集使用者的流程？如果是，请提供描述。我们鼓励对SA-V进行进一步标注，但这些标注不会由Meta验证/核实或支持/托管/维护。

items.en: At a high level, what are the subjective aspects of your task? Selecting objects to mask and track in a video is inherently a subjective task, and annotators might differ in their decision to mask objects.
items.zh: 从高层次看，你的任务有哪些主观方面？选择视频中要遮罩和跟踪的对象本质上是一个主观任务，标注者可能在是否遮罩对象上存在分歧。

items.en: What assumptions do you make about annotators? We assume our annotators understand the PVS task and are well trained on video related tasks. Our annotators worked full time on our annotation task. This made it possible to train the annotators by sharing feedback on a regular basis.
items.zh: 你对标注者做了哪些假设？我们假设标注者理解PVS任务，并接受过视频相关任务的良好培训。我们的标注者全职从事标注任务。这使得通过定期分享反馈来培训标注者成为可能。

items.en: How did you choose the specific wording of your task instructions? What steps, if any, were taken to verify the clarity of task instructions and wording for annotators? (1) The task instructions included visual examples (images and videos) to provide clarity. (2) Annotators were well trained before working on production queues. (3) The research team shared feedback daily and met with the annotators weekly for Q&A sessions.
items.zh: 你是如何选择任务指令的具体措辞的？采取了哪些步骤来验证任务指令和措辞对标注者的清晰度？（1）任务指令包含视觉示例（图像和视频）以提供清晰度。（2）标注者在进入生产队列前接受了良好培训。（3）研究团队每天分享反馈，每周与标注者开会进行问答。

items.en: What, if any, risks did your task pose for annotators and were they informed of the risks prior to engagement with the task? Annotators were informed to reject objectionable videos.
items.zh: 你的任务给标注者带来了哪些风险？在参与任务之前，他们是否被告知这些风险？标注者被告知要拒绝令人反感的视频。

items.en: What are the precise instructions that were provided to annotators? See detail in 11 for annotation instructions.
items.zh: 向标注员提供了哪些精确的指令？详见第11节的标注说明。

items.en: Are there certain perspectives that should be privileged? If so, how did you seek these perspectives out? We chose to work with annotators with previous video annotation experience.
items.zh: 是否有某些观点应被优先考虑？如果是，您如何寻求这些观点？我们选择与有先前视频标注经验的标注员合作。

items.en: Are there certain perspectives that would be harmful to include? If so, how did you screen these perspectives out? No.
items.zh: 是否有某些观点包含有害内容？如果是，您如何筛选这些观点？没有。

items.en: Were sociodemographic characteristics used to select annotators for your task? If so, please detail the process. For masklet annotations, sociodemographic characteristics were not used to select the annotators. For video collection, we emphasized the importance of diversity among the crowdworkers to our third-party vendor. While it was not a strict requirement, we encouraged the inclusion of a diverse group of crowdworkers to enrich the data collection process with a wide range of perspectives. This approach aimed to naturally incorporate diversity without imposing strict selection based on sociodemographic factors.
items.zh: 是否使用社会人口学特征来选择标注员？如果是，请详细说明过程。对于掩码标注，未使用社会人口学特征来选择标注员。对于视频收集，我们向第三方供应商强调了众包工人多样性的重要性。虽然这不是严格的要求，但我们鼓励纳入多样化的众包工人群体，以丰富数据收集过程，涵盖广泛的观点。这种方法旨在自然融入多样性，而不基于社会人口学因素进行严格筛选。

items.en: If you have any aggregated socio-demographic statistics about your annotator pool, please describe. Do you have reason to believe that sociodemographic characteristics of annotators may have impacted how they annotated the data? Why or why not? Aggregated socio-demographic statistics about the crowdworkers who collected the videos are presented in 5.2.
items.zh: 如果您有关于标注员群体的汇总社会人口学统计数据，请描述。您是否有理由相信标注员的社会人口学特征可能影响了他们标注数据的方式？为什么或为什么不？收集视频的众包工人的汇总社会人口学统计数据见5.2节。

items.en: Consider the intended context of use of the dataset and the individuals and communities that may be impacted by a model trained on this dataset. Are these communities represented in your annotator pool? The SA-V dataset is a geographically diverse, publicly available, video segmentation dataset, as discussed in 5.2. In addition, we analyze the responsible AI axes of a model trained on the dataset, as discussed in 6.1.3.
items.zh: 考虑数据集的预期使用环境以及可能受基于该数据集训练的模型影响的个人和社区。这些社区是否在您的标注员群体中得到代表？SA-V数据集是一个地理多样化的公开视频分割数据集，如5.2节所述。此外，我们分析了基于该数据集训练的模型的负责任AI轴，如6.1.3节所述。

items.en: What annotation platform did you utilize? At a high level, what considerations informed your decision to choose this platform? Did the chosen platform sufficiently meet the requirements you outlined for annotator pools? Are any aspects not covered? We used an internal annotation platform.
items.zh: 您使用了什么标注平台？从高层次看，哪些考虑因素促使您选择该平台？所选平台是否充分满足您对标注员群体的要求？是否有任何方面未覆盖？我们使用了内部标注平台。

items.en: What, if any, communication channels did your chosen platform offer to facilitate communication with annotators? How did this channel of communication influence the annotation process and/or resulting annotations? The research team shared feedback daily and met with the annotators weekly to align on the task instructions and expectations and to hold Q&A sessions. Outside of those sessions, annotators had access to a spreadsheet and chat group to facilitate communication with the research team.
items.zh: 您选择的平台提供了哪些沟通渠道来促进与标注员的沟通？这种沟通渠道如何影响标注过程和/或最终标注？研究团队每天分享反馈，每周与标注员开会，以统一任务说明和期望，并举行问答环节。在这些会议之外，标注员可以访问电子表格和聊天群组，以便与研究团队沟通。

items.en: How much were annotators compensated? Did you consider any particular pay standards, when determining their compensation? If so, please describe. (1) The video collecting crowdworkers were compensated with an hourly wage set by the vendor. (2) Annotators were compensated with an hourly wage set by the vendor.
items.zh: 标注员获得了多少报酬？在确定报酬时，您是否考虑了特定的薪酬标准？如果是，请描述。（1）视频收集众包工人按供应商设定的小时工资获得报酬。（2）标注员按供应商设定的小时工资获得报酬。

items.en: How do you define the quality of annotations in your context, and how did you assess the quality in the dataset you constructed? Annotators were required to follow a training before moving to production queues. Annotators followed a 2-day training session led by the vendor and then were asked to annotate jobs from a training queue. Annotators were able to move from training to production after the vendor Q&A team or the research team reviewed their work and assessed quality. On average, annotators spent 1 - 2 weeks in training before moving to production. Similarly, the vendor and research team Q&A manually reviewed the production queues’ annotations daily, sharing feedback daily.
items.zh: 您如何定义您上下文中的标注质量？您如何评估您构建的数据集中的质量？标注员在进入生产队列前必须接受培训。标注员参加了由供应商领导的为期2天的培训课程，然后被要求从培训队列中标注任务。在供应商问答团队或研究团队审查其工作并评估质量后，标注员才能从培训转入生产。平均而言，标注员在转入生产前需要1-2周的培训。同样，供应商和研究团队问答每天手动审查生产队列的标注，并每天分享反馈。

items.en: Have you conducted any analysis on disagreement patterns? If so, what analyses did you use and what were the major findings? Did you analyze potential sources of disagreement? The disagreement patterns were shared daily and weekly during feedback and Q&A sessions.
items.zh: 您是否对分歧模式进行了分析？如果是，您使用了哪些分析，主要发现是什么？您是否分析了分歧的潜在来源？在反馈和问答会议期间，每天和每周分享分歧模式。

items.en: How do the individual annotator responses relate to the final labels released in the dataset? The final labels are after data cleaning and post processing from the individual annotator responses.
items.zh: 个体标注员的响应与数据集中发布的最终标签有何关系？最终标签是在数据清理和后处理之后，从个体标注员的响应中得出的。

items.en: Do you have reason to believe the annotations in this dataset may change over time? Do you plan to update your dataset? No.
items.zh: 您是否有理由相信该数据集中的标注可能随时间变化？您计划更新您的数据集吗？没有。

items.en: Are there any conditions or definitions that, if changed, could impact the utility of your dataset? No.
items.zh: 是否有任何条件或定义，如果改变，可能影响您数据集的实用性？没有。

items.en: Will you attempt to track, impose limitations on, or otherwise influence how your dataset is used? If so, how? The SA-V dataset is released under a permissive CC by 4.0 license.
items.zh: 您是否会尝试跟踪、施加限制或以其他方式影响您的数据集的使用方式？如果是，如何？SA-V数据集在宽松的CC by 4.0许可下发布。

items.en: Were annotators informed about how the data is externalized? If changes to the dataset are made, will they be informed? No.
items.zh: 标注员是否被告知数据如何外部化？如果对数据集进行更改，是否会通知他们？没有。

items.en: Is there a process by which annotators can later choose to withdraw their data from the dataset? If so, please detail. No.
items.zh: 是否有流程允许标注员后来选择从数据集中撤回其数据？如果是，请详细说明。没有。
